100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 25, 1926, a general strike continued to grip Britain, with workers demanding better conditions and wages, significantly impacting industries across the nation. In the United States, the Senate faced heated debates over the controversial McFadden Bill aimed at regulating the banking sector. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions rose as the government struggled to stabilize the economy amid hyperinflation.
